info.title: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/operations/{operationId}` endpoint retrieves information on a specific operation performed in Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure.

parameters.name: operationId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned to a session of the operation in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

400.description: Bad Request. The request body is malformed, incomplete or otherwise invalid.

401.description: Unauthorized. The Authorization header does not contain an access token, or the provided access token is invalid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user sending a request does not have adequate privileges to access one or more objects specified in the request.

404.description: Not Found. One or more resources specified in the request could not be found in the specified resource collection.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/cloudInfrastructure/availabilitySets` endpoint retrieves a list of configured availability sets.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Specifies the first N items of a resource collection that will be excluded from the response.

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of items of a resource collection that will be returned in the response.

parameters.name: SubscriptionId
parameters.description: Returns only availability sets created in a subscription with the specified ID.

parameters.name: ResourceGroupName
parameters.description: Returns only availability sets that belong to a resource group with the specified name.

parameters.name: Region
parameters.description: Returns only availability sets that belong to an Azure region with the specified ID.

parameters.name: ServiceAccountId
parameters.description: Returns only availability sets to which a service account with the specified system ID has permissions.

400.description: Bad Request. The request body is malformed, incomplete or otherwise invalid.

401.description: Unauthorized. The Authorization header does not contain an access token, or the provided access token is invalid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user sending a request does not have adequate privileges to access one or more objects specified in the request.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/cloudInfrastructure/availabilityZones` endpoint retrieves a list of existing availability zones.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Specifies the first N items of a resource collection that will be excluded from the response.

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of items of a resource collection that will be returned in the response.

parameters.name: SubscriptionId
parameters.description: Returns only zones available in a subscription with the specified ID.

parameters.name: RegionId
parameters.description: Returns only availability zones that belong to an Azure region with the specified ID.

parameters.name: ServiceAccountId
parameters.description: Returns only availability zones to which a service account with the specified system ID has permissions.

parameters.name: VmSizeName
parameters.description: Returns only availability zones that support the specified size of Azure VMs.

401.description: Unauthorized. The Authorization header does not contain an access token, or the provided access token is invalid.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/accounts/azure/service` endpoint retrieves a list of service accounts added to the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure configuration database.

parameters.name: Filter

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Specifies the first N items of a resource collection that will be excluded from the response.

parameters.name: Limit

post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/accounts/azure/service/{accountId}/checkPermissions` checks whether the specified service account has all the permissions required to protect resources that belong to the specified Azure subscription.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API to the service account whose permissions will be checked.

parameters.name: subscriptionId
parameters.description: Specifies a Microsoft Azure ID assigned to a subscription whose resources will be protected using the permissions of the service account.

parameters.name: purposes
parameters.description: Specifies operations that will be performed using the service account.

post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/accounts/azure/service/exportPermissions` endpoint exports a .JSON file with the full list of permissions required to perform operations selected for the service account with the specified ID.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API to the service account that must be assigned the exported permissions.

parameters.name: subscriptionId
parameters.description: Specifies a Microsoft Azure ID assigned to a subscription whose resources will be protected using the exported permissions.

schema.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns the content of the exported list of service account permissions.

parameters.description: Specifies a Microsoft Azure ID assigned to a subscription whose resources will be protected using the exported permissions.

parameters.name: editAccountId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API to the service account that must be assigned the exported permissions.

post.description:
The HTTP POST request to the `/accounts/azure/service/exportPermissionsByToken` endpoint exports a .JSON file with the full list of permissions that must be assigned to a service account to protect Azure resources associated with the specified subscription.
NOTE
This operation requires authentication to the Microsoft Azure Cross-platform Command Line Interface (Azure CLI). That is why to complete the operation, you must obtain the value of the `accessTokenCache` parameter.

parameters.name: subscriptionId
parameters.description: Specifies a Microsoft Azure ID assigned to a subscription whose resources will be protected using the exported permissions.

schema.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns the content of the exported list of service account permissions.

schema.description: Specifies a media type of representation of the exported data. The default value is *text/csv* media type.
Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns the content of the exported list of service accounts.

post.summary: Get List of Tenants by Token
post.description:
The HTTP POST request to the `/accounts/azure/service/listTenantsByToken` endpoint retrieves a list of Microsoft Entra tenants to which the Microsoft Azure account used to authenticate to the Azure CLI has access.
NOTE
This operation requires authentication to the Microsoft Azure Cross-platform Command Line Interface (Azure CLI). That is why to complete the operation, you must obtain the value of the `accessTokenCache` parameter.

400.description: Bad Request. The request body is malformed, incomplete or otherwise invalid.

401.description: Unauthorized. The Authorization header does not contain an access token, or the provided access token is invalid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user sending a request does not have adequate privileges to access one or more objects specified in the request.

404.description: Not Found. One or more resources specified in the request could not be found in the specified resource collection.

post.summary: Get List of Subscriptions by Application
post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/accounts/azure/service/listSubscriptionsByApp` retrieves a list of subscriptions available for the service account connected to the existing Entra ID application.

parameters.name: editAccountId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API to the service account that will be edited.

400.description: Bad Request. The request body is malformed, incomplete or otherwise invalid.

401.description: Unauthorized. The Authorization header does not contain an access token, or the provided access token is invalid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user sending a request does not have adequate privileges to access one or more objects specified in the request.

post.summary: Get List of Microsoft Entra Groups
post.description:
The HTTP POST request to the `/accounts/azure/service/listAdGroups` endpoint retrieves a list of Microsoft Entra groups available in the Microsoft Azure environment.
NOTE
This operation requires authentication to the Microsoft Azure Cross-platform Command Line Interface (Azure CLI). That is why to complete the operation, you must obtain the value of the `accessTokenCache` parameter.

400.description: Bad Request. The request body is malformed, incomplete or otherwise invalid.

401.description: Unauthorized. The Authorization header does not contain an access token, or the provided access token is invalid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user sending a request does not have adequate privileges to access one or more objects specified in the request.

404.description: Not Found. One or more resources specified in the request could not be found in the specified resource collection.

post.description:
The HTTP POST request to the `/accounts/azure/service/isUserOwnerOfAdApp` endpoint checks whether the Microsoft Azure account authenticated to the Microsoft Azure Cross-platform Command Line Interface (Azure CLI) is the owner of the specified Entra ID application.
NOTE
This operation requires authentication to the Microsoft Azure Cross-platform Command Line Interface (Azure CLI). That is why to complete the operation, you must obtain the value of the `accessTokenCache` parameter.

parameters.name: applicationId
parameters.description: Specifies the Microsoft Azure ID assigned to the Entra ID application.

parameters.name: editAccountId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API to the service account with which the specified Entra ID application is associated.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Specifies the Microsoft Azure ID assigned to a tenant in which the specified Entra ID application has been registered.

schema.description: Defines whether the authenticated user is the owner of the Entra ID application.

post.summary: Add Service Account Using Existing Application
post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/accounts/azure/service/saveByApp` endpoint creates a new service account using an existing Entra ID application.

schema.description: System ID assigned to the created account in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

put.summary: Edit Service Account Created Using Existing Application
put.description: The HTTP PUT request to the `/accounts/azure/service/updateByApp/{accountId}` endpoint updates a specific service account created using an existing Entra ID application.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned to a service account in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

schema.description: System ID assigned to the edited account in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

put.summary: Validate Editing of Service Account Created Using Existing Application
put.description: The HTTP PUT request to the `/accounts/azure/service/updateByApp/{accountId}/validate` endpoint checks whether the data specified for the HTTP PUT request to the [Edit Service Account Created Using Existing Application](#tag/AzureServiceAccount/operation/AzureServiceAccount_UpdateByApp) endpoint is valid.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned to a service account in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

get.summary: Get Service Accounts Suitable for Worker Management
get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/accounts/azure/service/listWorkerManagementSuitableServices` endpoint retrieves a list of service accounts added to the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure configuration database that can be used to launch worker instances.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: Returns only specific items of a resource collection in the response. To learn how to use the `filter` parameter, see section [Filter Parameters](https://helpcenter.veeam.com/references/vbazure/8.1/rest/main/tag/SectionOverview#section/Query-Parameters/Filter-Parameters).

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Specifies the first N items of a resource collection that will be excluded from the response.

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of items of a resource collection that will be returned in the response.

401.description: Unauthorized. The Authorization header does not contain an access token, or the provided access token is invalid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user sending a request does not have adequate privileges to access one or more objects specified in the request.

put.summary: Specify Service Account for Worker Management
put.description: The HTTP PUT request to the `/accounts/azure/service/{accountId}/selectForWorkermanagement` endpoint allows you to specify a service account that will be used to launch worker instances.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned to a service account in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/settings/certificates` endpoint retrieves TLS certificates used to establish secure communication between the web browser and the backup appliance or worker instances.

post.description:
The HTTP POST request to the `/settings/certificates/webserver/regenerate/` endpoint replaces an existing security certificate with a new self-signed certificate automatically generated by Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure.
NOTE
After you replace an existing security certificate, the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API and Web UI services will be restarted automatically.

put.description: The HTTP PUT request to the `/settings/certificates/webServer` endpoint updates a web certificate.

post.summary: Calculate Estimated Cost of Azure VM Schedule-Based Backup Policy
post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/policies/virtualMachines/estimateCost` endpoint calculates the estimated monthly cost for protecting resources by a backup policy per Azure VM.

parameters.name: VirtualMachineNameFilter
parameters.description: Returns only costs for protecting an Azure VM with the specified name.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Specifies the first N items of a resource collection that will be excluded from the response.

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of items of a resource collection that will be returned in the response.

post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/policies/virtualMachines/estimateCost/export` endpoint exports the estimation cost report to a .CSV or an .XML file.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Specifies a media type of representation of the exported data. The default value is *text/csv* media type.

schema.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns the content of the exported cost estimation report.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/databases` endpoint retrieves all SQL databases that can be protected by Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Specifies the first N items of a resource collection that will be excluded from the response.

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of items of a resource collection that will be returned in the response.

parameters.name: SubscriptionId
parameters.description: Returns only SQL databases available in a subscription with the specified ID.

parameters.name: TenantId
parameters.description: Returns only SQL databases available for a tenant with the specified ID.

parameters.name: ServiceAccountId
parameters.description: Returns only SQL databases to which a service account with the specified ID has access.

parameters.name: SearchPattern
parameters.description: Returns only those items of a resource collection whose names match the specified search pattern in the parameter value.

parameters.name: CredentialsState
parameters.description: Returns only SQL databases that have the specified credentials state.

parameters.name: AssignableBySqlAccountId
parameters.description: Returns only SQL databases that are associated with an Azure SQL account with the specified ID.

parameters.name: RegionIds
parameters.description: Returns only SQL databases that reside in Azure regions with the specified IDs.

parameters.name: ProtectionStatus
parameters.description: Returns only SQL databases with the specified protection status.

parameters.name: BackupDestination
parameters.description: Returns only SQL databases with the specified backup type.

parameters.name: DbFromProtectedRegions
parameters.description: Defines whether Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure must return only SQL databases that reside in regions protected by backup policies.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/databases/{databaseId}` endpoint retrieves information on a SQL database with the specified ID.

parameters.name: databaseId
parameters.description: Specifies a system ID assigned in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API to the SQL database.

parameters.name: sync
parameters.description: Defines whether to force rescanning of the Microsoft Azure infrastructure before the response.

post.description: The HTTP POST requests to the `/databases/rescan` endpoint launches a rescan operation for SQL databases in Microsoft Azure environment.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Specifies the first N items of a resource collection that will be excluded from the response.

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of items of a resource collection that will be returned in the response.

post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/databases/export` endpoint exports a list of Azure SQL databases to a .CSV or an .XML file.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Specifies a media type of representation of the exported data. The default value is *text/csv* media type.

schema.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns the content of the exported list of Azure SQL databases.

post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/databases/startManualBackup` endpoint launches a backup operation for the specified databases.

202.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns the session ID of the requested operation. For the session log details, see [Job Sessions](#tag/JobSessions/operation/JobSessions_GetJobSessionLog).

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/configurationBackup/stats` endpoint retrieves statistics on Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure entities currently present in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure configuration database.

post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `configurationBackup/backup` endpoint creates a manual backup of the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure configuration database.

202.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns the session ID of the requested operation. For the session log details, see [Job Sessions](#tag/JobSessions/operation/JobSessions_GetJobSessionLog).

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/configurationBackup/settings` endpoint retrieves settings configured to perform backup of the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure configuration database.

put.description: The HTTP PUT request to the `/configurationBackup/settings` endpoint changes settings configured to perform backup of the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure configuration database.

post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `configurationBackup/export/start` endpoint launches an export operation for a specific configuration backup file.

202.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns the session ID of the requested operation. For the session log details, see [Job Sessions](#tag/JobSessions/operation/JobSessions_GetJobSessionLog).

get.summary: Check Status of Configuration Backup Export
get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `configurationBackup/export/status/{sessionId}` endpoint allows you to check whether the exported configuration backup file is ready for download.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned to an export session in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `configurationBackup/export/download` endpoint allows a user to download the exported configuration backup file.

200.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns a link to download the exported configuration backup file.

schema.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns the content of the exported configuration backup file.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/configurationBackup/restorePoints` endpoint retrieves a list of restore points created for the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure configuration database.

parameters.name: RepositoryId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned in the Veeam backup for Microsoft Azure REST API to a backup repository in which configuration backup files are stored.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Specifies the first N items of a resource collection that will be excluded from the response.

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of items of a resource collection that will be returned in the response.

get.summary: Get Configuration Backup Restore Point Data
get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/configurationBackup/restorePoints/{restorePointId}` endpoint retrieves information on a specific restore point created for the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure configuration database.

parameters.name: restorePointId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned to a restore point in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

delete.description: The HTTP DELETE request to the `/configurationBackup/restorePoints/{restorePointId}` endpoint deletes a specific restore point created for the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure configuration database.

parameters.name: restorePointId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned to a restore point in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

202.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns the session ID of the requested operation. For the session log details, see [Job Sessions](#tag/JobSessions/operation/JobSessions_GetJobSessionLog).

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/restorePoints/fileShares` endpoint retrieves a list of restore points created for Azure file shares and available for Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure.

parameters.name: FileShareId
parameters.description: Returns only restore points of an Azure file share with the specified ID.

parameters.name: OnlyLatest
parameters.description: Defines whether to return only recently created restore points.

parameters.name: PointInTime
parameters.description: Returns only restore points created on the specified date and time.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Specifies the first N items of a resource collection that will be excluded from the response.

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of items of a resource collection that will be returned in the response.

get.summary: Get File Share Restore Point Data
get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/restorePoints/fileShares/{restorePointId}` endpoint retrieves information on a specific restore point of an Azure file share.

parameters.name: restorePointId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned to a restore point in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

delete.description: The HTTP DELETE request to the `/restorePoints/fileShares/{restorePointId}` endpoint removes a specific restore point of an Azure file share from the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure configuration database.

parameters.name: restorePointId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned to a restore point in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

202.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns the session ID of the requested operation. For the session log details, see [Job Sessions](#tag/JobSessions/operation/JobSessions_GetJobSessionLog).

post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/restorePoints/fileShares/{restorePointId}/restoreFile` endpoint launches a file-level restore operation using a specific restore point of an Azure file share.

parameters.name: restorePointId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned to a restore point in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

202.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns the session ID of the requested operation. For the session log details, see [Job Sessions](#tag/JobSessions/operation/JobSessions_GetJobSessionLog).

post.summary: Perform File Share Restore Point Indexing
post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/restorePoints/fileShares/startIndexing` endpoint allows you to instruct Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure to perform indexing for the restore points with the specified IDs.

202.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns the session ID of the requested operation. For the session log details, see [Job Sessions](#tag/JobSessions/operation/JobSessions_GetJobSessionLog).

get.summary: Get Virtual Network Configuration Restore Points
get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/restorePoints/vnets` endpoint retrieves a list of restore points created for virtual network configurations in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure configuration database.

parameters.name: RegionId
parameters.description: Returns only restore points created in Azure regions with the specified IDs.

parameters.name: SubscriptionId
parameters.description: Returns only restore points created for Azure subscriptions with the specified IDs.

parameters.name: BackupDestinations
parameters.description: Returns only restore points with the specified backup destinations.

parameters.name: OnlyLatest
parameters.description: Defines whether to return only recently created restore points.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Specifies the first N items that will be excluded from the response.

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of items that will be returned in the response.

get.summary: Get Virtual Network Configuration Restore Point Data
get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/restorePoints/vnets/{restorePointId}` endpoint retrieves information on a specific virtual network configuration restore point created for the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure configuration database.

parameters.name: restorePointId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned to a restore point in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

get.summary: Compare Virtual Network Configuration Restore Point Backups
get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/restorePoints/vnets/{restorePointId}/compareWithProduction` endpoint allows you to compare the current Azure virtual network configuration to the backed-up virtual network configuration data from the restore point with the specified ID.

parameters.name: restorePointId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned to a restore point in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

parameters.name: OnlyDifferent
parameters.description: Defines whether to return only backed-up virtual network configuration items that differ from the current virtual network configuration items.

parameters.name: RegionId
parameters.description: Returns only results for an Azure region with the specified ID.

post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/restorePoints/vnets/{restorePointId}/restore` endpoint restores virtual network configuration items using the restore point with the specified ID.

parameters.name: restorePointId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned to a restore point in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

202.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure returns the session ID of the requested operation. For the session log details, see [Job Sessions](#tag/JobSessions/operation/JobSessions_GetJobSessionLog).

get.summary: Get Virtual Network Configuration Restore Point Regions by Subscription
get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/restorePoints/vnetRegions/{subscriptionId}` endpoint retrieves all Azure regions where Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure created virtual network configuration restore points for an Azure subscription with the specified ID.

parameters.name: subscriptionId
parameters.description: Specifies the system ID assigned to a subscription in the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Specifies the first N items that will be excluded from the response.

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of items that will be returned in the response.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/jobSessions` endpoint retrieves a list of Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure sessions.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Specifies the first N items of a resource collection that will be excluded from the response.

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of items of a resource collection that will be returned in the response.

parameters.name: PolicyId
parameters.description: Returns only sessions of a backup policy with the specified ID.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: Returns only sessions that match the specified filter in the parameter value.

parameters.name: Status
parameters.description: Returns only sessions with the specified status.

parameters.name: Types
parameters.description: Returns only sessions of the specified types.

parameters.name: FromUtc
parameters.description: Returns only sessions launched after the specified date and time. **Note**: The property value must be specified in the UTC time zone in the following format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

parameters.name: ToUtc
parameters.description: Returns only sessions launched before the specified date and time. **Note**: The property value must be specified in the UTC time zone in the following format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/license` endpoint retrieves information on the installed Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure license.

delete.description: The HTTP DELETE request to the `/license/licensingServer` endpoint removes the backup appliance from the Veeam Backup & Replication license.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/licenseAgreement` endpoint retrieves information on license agreements and checksum parameters required for accepting terms of the agreements.
The HTTP GET request to the `/licenseAgreement/accepted` endpoint verifies whether license agreements were accepted.

schema.description: Defines whether license agreements were accepted.

post.description:
The HTTP POST request to the `/licenseAgreement/accept` endpoint allows you to accept terms of the license agreements.
NOTE
To read the specific agreement and get the parameters `checksum` and `type` required for accepting that agreement, send the HTTP GET request to the [Get License Agreements](#tag/LicenseAgreement/operation/LicenseAgreement_GetLicenseAgreement) endpoint.

schema.description: Specifies a list of documents to be accepted.

post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/api/oauth2/token` endpoint allows you to authorize your access to the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API.

delete.description: The HTTP DELETE request to the `/api/oauth2/token` endpoint performs the logout operation for an authorized user.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/system/about` endpoint retrieves Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure system information.

The HTTP GET request to the `/system/time` endpoint retrieves the current time zone in which backup appliance is operating.
The HTTP GET request to the `/system/timezones` endpoint retrieves a list of time zones that can be set for the backup appliance.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/system/status` endpoint retrieves the status of backup appliance after update.
The HTTP GET request to the `/system/supportInfo` endpoint retrieves the tenant ID and a support code.
The HTTP GET request to the `/system/updaterLink` endpoint retrieves the URL of the updater service.

schema.description: URL of the updater service.

Note that the service account specified in the *azureAccountId* parameter must have the worker management permissions assigned in this subscription. get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/overview/sessionsSummary` endpoint retrieves session statistics for the past 24 hours.

The HTTP GET request to the `/overview/statistics` endpoint retrieves statistics on the number of snapshots, backups and archived backups successfully created by backup policies during a specific time period.

parameters.name: PeriodFlag
parameters.description: Specifies the time period for which the statistics must be collected. The default period is the past 24 hours.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/overview/protectedWorkloads` endpoint retrieves statistics on the number of available Azure resources that got protected by Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure during a specific time period.

parameters.name: PeriodFlag
parameters.description: Specifies the time period for which the statistics must be collected. The default period is the past 24 hours.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/overview/storageUsage` endpoint retrieves statistics on the amount of storage space that is currently consumed by backups and archived backups created by Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure in blob containers, and the number of snapshots created for the protected resources.
The HTTP GET request to the `/overview/topPoliciesDuration` endpoint retrieves top N backup policies for fluctuations in execution time (including retries).

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Specifies the top N number of backup policies that will be returned in the response.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/overview/bottlenecks` endpoint retrieves information on possible backup bottlenecks.
The HTTP GET request to the `/workers/customTags` endpoint retrieves a list of custom tags that are assigned to worker instances.

put.description: The HTTP PUT request to the `/workers/customTags` endpoint updates a list of custom tags that must be assigned to worker instances.

post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/configuration/check` endpoint runs the configuration check operation for the specified check types.

get.description: The HTTP GET request ot the `/configuration/checkSession` endpoint retrieves results of the configuration check operation.

post.description:
The HTTP POST request to the `/configuration/overrideOwnership` endpoint allows you to override the owner for the backup repositories with the specified IDs to the current backup appliance. To be able to override the owner for a backup repository, you must first add this repository to the current backup appliance by sending the HTTP POST request to the [Add Backup Repository](#tag/Repository/operation/Repository_CreateRepository) endpoint.
NOTE
As soon as you override a repository owner, backup policies configured on the backup appliance to which the repository previously belonged will start failing.

post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/veeamVaults/oauth2/completeSignIn` endpoint allows you to generate a token that you can use to register your backup appliance in Veeam Data Cloud Vault.

get.summary: Get Veeam Data Cloud Vault Registration Information
get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/veeamVaults/registration` endpoint retrieves information on whether your backup appliance is registered in Veeam Data Cloud Vault.

post.description:
The HTTP POST request to the `/veeamVaults/registration` endpoint allows you to register your backup appliance in Veeam Data Cloud Vault.
NOTE
To complete this operation, obtain the value of the `token` parameter. To do that, send the HTTP POST request to the [Generate Veeam Data Cloud Vault Registration Token](#tag/VeeamVaults/operation/VeeamVaults_CompleteSignIn) endpoint.

get.description: The HTTP GET request to the `/veeamVaults` endpoint retrieves a list of storage vaults connected to your backup appliance in Veeam Data Cloud Vault.

post.description: The HTTP POST request to the `/veeamVaults/importAccount` endpoint imports data of a specific storage vault from Veeam Data Cloud to the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure configuration database. You can then use the imported data as an input in the HTTP POST request to the [Add Backup Repository](#tag/Repository/operation/Repository_CreateRepository) endpoint.

PrivateDnsSetting.description:
Specifies the private DNS zone settings:
  • If you select the *Disabled* value, you must do the following: create the DNS zones, add to the DNS zones A records for the private endpoints of disk access resources and Veeam storage accounts, and add to the DNS zones the VNet to which the backup appliance is connected and the VNet to which worker instances are connected.
  • If you select the *ManageARecords* value, you must create the DNS zones and add to these DNS zones the VNet to which the backup appliance is connected and the VNet to which worker instances are connected, while Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure will automatically add to the DNS zones A records for the private endpoint of disk access resources and Veeam storage accounts.
  • If you select the *ManageAutomatically* value, Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure will automatically create the DNS zones in the resource group to which worker instances belong, add to these DNS zones A records for the private endpoint of disk access resources and Veeam storage accounts, and add to the DNS zones the VNet to which the backup appliance is connected and the VNet to which worker instances are connected.
NOTE
Regardless of the value you selected for the `PrivateDnsSetting` parameter, you must configure peering connections between the VNet to which the backup appliance is connected and the VNet to which worker instances are connected. For more information, see the Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure User Guide.

StorageAccountPublicAccess.description:
Specifies the Veeam storage account settings:
  • If you select the *Enabled* value, the Veeam storage accounts will have public network access enabled.
  • If you select the *Disabled* value, the Veeam storage accounts will have public network access disabled.
  • If you select the *DoNotModify* value, Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure will keep the existing network configuration of the Veeam storage accounts.

maxInstances.description:
Maximum number of worker instances that can be deployed in the specified region and used simultaneously to process Azure resources during backup and restore operations.
**Note**: After a backup or restore operation completes, Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure keeps the minimum number of worker instances running for 10 minutes and then deallocates them; other instances are automatically removed from the backup infrastructure.

virtualMachineLargeType.description: Profile used to create image-level backups and restore data if the total disk size of the processed Azure VM or the total size of the processed Azure SQL database is more than 1 TB.

tenantId.description: Specifies the Microsoft Azure ID assigned to a tenant in which worker instances will be launched. If no value is specified for the property, Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure will use the tenant where the backup appliance is deployed. Consider that if private network deployment is enabled on the backup appliance, you can only specify a tenant where the backup appliance is deployed.

subscriptionId.description: Specifies the Microsoft Azure ID assigned to a subscription in which worker instances will be launched. Note that the subscription must belong to the tenant specified in the value of the `tenantId` property. If no value is specified for the `subscriptionId` property, Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure will use the subscription where the backup appliance is deployed. Consider that if private network deployment is enabled on the backup appliance, you can only specify a subscription belonging to the tenant where the backup appliance is deployed.

resourceGroup.description: Specifies the name of the resource group in which the worker instances will be launched. Note that the resource group must belong to the subscription specified in the `subscriptionId` property. If no value is specified for the `resourceGroup` property, Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure will automatically generate the value and create a resource group in the subscription specified in the `subscriptionId` property, or in the subscription where the backup appliance is deployed.

minInstances.description: Specifies number of worker instances that are deployed in the specified region and kept in the Azure environment even when no operations are performed by Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure.

maxInstances.description:
Specifies a maximum number of worker instances that can be deployed in the specified region and used simultaneously to process Azure resources during backup and restore operations.
**Note**: After a backup or restore operation completes, Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure keeps the minimum number of worker instances running for 10 minutes and then deallocates them; the other instances are automatically removed from the backup infrastructure.

createEvery.description: Specifies the interval (in number of time units) at which the restore points will be created. **Note**: For the `createUnit` parameter, you can only specify either *Minute* or *Hour*. If you specify *Minute* for the `createUnit` parameter, you can only use either of the following values for the `createEvery` parameter: 5, 10, 15, 20, 30; if you specify *Hour* for the `createUnit` parameter, you can only use either of the following values for the `createEvery` parameter: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 24.

storeFor.description: Specifies the time period (in number of time units) during which Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure will keep the created restore points. **Note**: For the `createUnit` parameter, you can specify the following values only: either *Day* or *Month* for a snapshot schedule, and *Hour* for a backup schedule.

tags.x-displayName: About Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API
tags.description:
Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure exposes its entities through an application programming interface (API) based on the Representational State Transfer (REST) architecture style and Open Data Protocol (OData). The REST API allows developers to query information about Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure entities and perform basic operations with these entities using HTTP requests and standard [HTTP methods](SectionOverview#section/HTTP-Methods).

The Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API is a resource-based application programming interface that uses [Hypertext Application Language (HAL)](https://stateless.group/hal_specification.html). HAL expresses the resources and relationships between them as hyperlinks, and enables navigation through the API. All individual resources have a JSON representation and system resource identifiers to help you locate a resource by its URL. Resources of the same entity type comprise a collection. A collection is modeled as an array of objects. Every collection includes hyperlinks that end with resource identifiers and point at its individual resources, related resources and collections.

The Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API relies on the client-server model. The client makes a request to the server; the server processes the request and returns a response with a conventional [HTTP response code](http://www.ietf.org/assignments/http-status-codes/http-status-codes.xml), HTTP response headers and a response body in the JSON format (by default).

The Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API is served over HTTPS. To ensure data privacy, unencrypted HTTP is not supported.


### REST API Reference Map

For visual representation of all operations available in REST API v8, see the [Veeam Backup for Microsoft Azure REST API Reference Map](https://www.veeam.com/veeam_backup_azure_8_rest_api_reference_map_ot.pdf).
